content: 2024–2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be rearchitected to treat each individual general agent within a multi-agent ecosystem as a differentiated, retrievable entry in the active learning memory storage. This lets the collective system actively query only the most informative agents for new task-solving, rather than wasting compute on redundant or unhelpful agent responses, while updating the memory of which agents are most useful for future tasks.
